quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian shepherds developed the Puli dog breed with its distinctive corded coat that resembles dreadlocks. These intelligent dogs were so valued that a shepherd would pay a full year's salary for a good Puli! The cords naturally form as the puppy grows and help protect the dog from harsh weather and predators.

short_read.title: Harbor Seals: Masters of the Pacific Northwest Coast
short_read.teaser: These charming marine mammals are frequently spotted lounging on rocks throughout the Strait of Juan de Fuca. Learn about their fascinating adaptations and behaviors that make them so successful in local waters.

medium_read.title: The Secret Life of Trees: How They Communicate Underground
medium_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that trees in forests are connected by vast underground fungal networks, allowing them to share nutrients and warnings. This "wood wide web" reveals that forests are far more social than we ever imagined.

long_read.title: The Danube: Europe's Great River of Culture and History
long_read.teaser: From Germany's Black Forest to the Black Sea, the Danube has shaped civilizations for millennia, flowing through Hungary's heart. This journey explores the river's role in art, music, and the daily lives of millions along its banks.

port_angeles.title: Ediz Hook's Resident Bald Eagles
port_angeles.description: The Ediz Hook wildlife refuge is home to nesting bald eagles that can often be spotted fishing in the harbor waters. June is an excellent time to see adult eagles teaching their young to hunt, as fledglings take their first flights. The three-mile sandbar also offers stunning views of the Strait and Olympic Mountains.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! You know your hummingbird facts! Their tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power those amazing wings.
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! These tiny hearts are remarkable machines, beating around 1,200 times per minute in flight!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These tiny hearts are incredible — they beat about 1,200 times per minute during flight! That's about 20 beats per second.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any animal on Earth. At rest, their heart rate slows to about 250 beats per minute, and they enter a hibernation-like state called torpor at night to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound lovely and delicate, just like the creature itself?
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, belonging to the Finno-Ugric family rather than Indo-European languages. It's known for its beautiful, flowing sounds and is considered one of the most poetic languages in the world.

puzzle_3.question: What type of whale is most commonly seen in the Strait of Juan de Fuca during summer months?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Orcas are regular visitors to these waters, especially in summer when salmon are running. You might even spot them from the shore on a lucky day!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The orcas, or killer whales, are the most commonly seen! These magnificent creatures visit the Strait regularly during summer months to hunt salmon.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Southern Resident orcas are actually an endangered population that frequents the Strait of Juan de Fuca. These highly intelligent and social animals live in family groups called pods and can live 50-80 years in the wild.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
